steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage records 6,000 M. robustus individuals at the Octopus Meadow: adults, hatchlings, and eggs — but no juveniles. The correct answer must explain this pattern using only what the data shows.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The *M. robustus* population at the Octopus Meadow remains stable despite variations in water temperature.: claims population stability despite temperature changes. The passage doesn't compare population size across temperatures — this is unsupported — eliminate. ✗
  • Choice B — The Octopus Meadow provides an ideal feeding ground for *M. robustus* hatchlings.: describes the meadow as a feeding ground. The passage gives no evidence of feeding behavior there — eliminate. ✗
  • Choice C — *M. robustus* nests in the Octopus Meadow contain on average fewer but larger eggs than nests at similar ocean depths.: makes a comparison about egg size. No egg-size comparison appears in the passage — eliminate. ✗
  • Choice D — *M. robustus* leave the Octopus Meadow upon reaching an intermediary stage of development.: states M. robustus leave the meadow upon reaching an intermediary stage of development (juvenility). Hatchlings and adults are present; juveniles are absent — they must exit during the juvenile stage. Directly supported by the data. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
